events.title: Israel Continues Strikes on Lebanon Amidst Iran Ceasefire
events.desc: Despite a halt in direct hostilities with Iran, Israel continues its military operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. The Lebanese health ministry reported at least 14 fatalities on Monday, including paramedics, highlighting the ongoing conflict despite a US-brokered partial ceasefire aimed at de-escalating tensions in the region.

events.title: US Warns Netanyahu Against Renewed War with Iran
events.desc: US President Donald Trump reportedly warned Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu against re-engaging in conflict with Iran, stating that Israel might find itself fighting alone. This warning comes amidst ongoing tensions and sporadic exchanges of fire in the region, complicating diplomatic efforts to maintain a ceasefire.

events.title: Lebanese President Appeals for Talks Over War with Israel
events.desc: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun directly appealed to the Israeli government and its people to pursue negotiations rather than continued conflict. He emphasized that a military solution would not yield lasting security and expressed Lebanon's readiness for dialogue.

events.title: Israeli Minister Under Investigation in Italy Over Gaza Flotilla
events.desc: Italian prosecutors have launched an investigation into Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir concerning the treatment of activists from a Gaza flotilla. He is being investigated on suspicion of torture and kidnapping of Italian citizens involved in the event.

events.title: Iran Threatens to Defeat US Naval Blockade
events.desc: Iran's top negotiator, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, stated that Iran would turn the US naval blockade into another 'defeat for the enemy'. He indicated a strategy of selective engagement, fighting and negotiating on Iran's own terms, with the ultimate goal of ending the war and achieving stable security.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Despite Truce
events.desc: Israel conducted airstrikes on suburbs of Beirut, stating the action was a response to Hezbollah's cross-border fire. This escalation occurred days after a US-brokered truce aimed at de-escalating the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: US Military Aided Israel in Intercepting Iranian Missiles
events.desc: The US military provided assistance to Israel in intercepting Iranian missile attacks. These exchanges followed Israeli airstrikes on the outskirts of Beirut, despite the White House having publicly expressed opposition to such actions.

events.title: Bahrain Bans Mourning of Iran's Khamenei Amid Regional Tensions
events.desc: Bahrain has prohibited public mourning for Iran's recently deceased leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. This move reflects heightened tensions in the Gulf region, exacerbated by recent Iranian attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait, and the renewed conflict between Iran and Israel.

events.title: Iran and Israel Agree to Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Iran and Israel have announced a temporary pause in their military operations, with leaders from both nations warning of renewed retaliation if the ceasefire is violated. Israel's Prime Minister stated that the country is holding fire 'at the moment', following a similar announcement from Iran's armed forces.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill 11 in Gaza City
events.desc: Medical officials reported that Israeli airstrikes on four residential buildings in Gaza City resulted in the deaths of 11 people, including women and children. The Israeli military stated that the strikes targeted four Hamas security officials.

events.title: Iranian Drone Strikes Hit Kuwait Airport, Causing Casualties
events.desc: Iran claimed responsibility for drone attacks on Kuwait International Airport, stating the action was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and a strategic island. The attack resulted in one fatality and dozens of injuries.

events.title: US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites Amid Kuwait Attacks
events.desc: The United States confirmed strikes on Iranian radar sites in response to Iran's drone attacks targeting American forces in Kuwait. Both nations reported fresh military actions in the vicinity of the Strait of Hormuz, with Kuwait condemning the repeated Iranian assaults.

events.title: Lebanese General Among Three Killed in Israeli Car Attack
events.desc: An Israeli military operation targeting a vehicle in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of three soldiers, including a Lebanese general. The Israeli military has initiated an investigation into the incident, which occurred amid ongoing clashes with Hezbollah.

events.title: Clashes Persist in Lebanon Despite Partial Ceasefire
events.desc: Fighting continues in southern Lebanon between Hezbollah and Israeli forces, even as both sides have reportedly accepted a US-brokered partial ceasefire plan. The cessation of hostilities is seen as critical for the broader peace process involving Iran.

events.title: US Lists Chinese Tech Companies as 'Military Companies'
events.desc: The United States has designated Chinese technology firms BYD, Alibaba, and Baidu as 'Chinese military companies.' The Chinese embassy in Washington has condemned this move, labeling it as 'discriminatory.' This action could lead to further sanctions and restrictions on these entities.

events.title: Global Nuclear Weapons Spending Rises Sharply
events.desc: Global spending on nuclear weapons by the nine nuclear-armed states increased by nearly 20% in 2025, reaching $119 billion. This marks the highest expenditure since tracking began in 2020, with the United States leading the increase. The report was released by the International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been leaked online, exposing security vulnerabilities across various ministries. A Bellingcat analysis indicated that 12 out of 13 ministries were affected, potentially compromising sensitive information.
